skill.name: uvicorn
skill.description: ASGI server for Python web applications - Fast, production-ready server for async frameworks
skill.summary: ASGI server for Python web applications - Fast, production-ready server for async frameworks
skill.author: bossjones

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, network, filesystem, credential, and obfuscation patterns, but review found documentation examples rather than executable skill code. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or hidden runtime behavior was found. The remaining risk is low because examples bind Uvicorn to all interfaces and should be used with deployment context.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported command execution locations are Markdown and JSON documentation examples for running Uvicorn. No executable script, dynamic shell call, or user-controlled command construction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced documentation examples or JSON text, not active code. I found no evidence of a runtime command execution path.

low_findings.title: Public Bind Examples Need Deployment Context
low_findings.description: Several examples use 0.0.0.0 for ASGI server binding. This is legitimate for containers and production routing, but copying it into local development can expose an application on all interfaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network binding examples are real and intentional Uvicorn usage. The risk depends on user deployment context, so this is a low operational warning rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Placeholder TLS And Socket Paths Are Not Credential Access
low_findings.description: The SSL key, certificate, and Unix socket paths are illustrative configuration examples. No actual secret material, key content, or file-reading behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited paths are placeholders inside documentation. I found no evidence that the skill reads, copies, or transmits certificate or key files.

content.value_statement: Python web teams need reliable guidance for running ASGI applications in development and production. This skill explains Uvicorn commands, worker settings, SSL options, Docker usage, and troubleshooting steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to run ASGI applications with Uvicorn.
Shows development setup with reload behavior.
Describes host, port, workers, logging, and factory options.
Covers HTTPS configuration with key and certificate file paths.
Provides Dockerfile and Docker Compose deployment patterns.
Lists common troubleshooting checks for ports, modules, reload, and workers.
limitations: It is documentation-only and does not execute deployment commands.
It assumes Uvicorn and application dependencies are installed separately.
It does not audit the security of the user's ASGI application.
It does not replace platform-specific production hardening guidance.

use_cases.title: Start local ASGI development
use_cases.description: Use concise Uvicorn guidance for FastAPI, Starlette, or custom ASGI applications.

use_cases.title: Plan production server settings
use_cases.description: Review worker, logging, host, port, SSL, and process manager options before deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare Docker runtime examples
use_cases.description: Adapt documented Dockerfile and Compose patterns for containerized ASGI services.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Local App
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a local ASGI application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to run my ASGI app with Uvicorn for local development.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Reload
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain when to use Uvicorn reload and what settings I should avoid in production.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Settings
prompt_templates.scenario: Production planning for an ASGI service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose Uvicorn host, port, workers, logging, and SSL settings for production.

prompt_templates.title: Design Container Deployment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced Docker and Compose deployment review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a deployment checklist for running Uvicorn in Docker with safe network and reload settings.

output_examples.input: How should I run a FastAPI app while I am editing files?
output: Use Uvicorn in development mode with reload enabled.
Keep the service bound locally unless another machine or container must reach it.
Do not combine reload mode with multiple production workers.

output_examples.input: What should I check before using Uvicorn in production?
output: Confirm worker count, logging level, host binding, port routing, and SSL termination.
Use a process manager or platform supervisor for restart behavior.
Keep reload disabled and review exposure before binding to all interfaces.

output_examples.input: What does this skill provide for Docker deployments?
output: It outlines the important Docker runtime choices for ASGI applications.
It explains why container services often bind to all interfaces.
It highlights reload and volume patterns that are useful only for development.

best_practices: Use reload only for local development workflows.
Bind to all interfaces only when containers or routing require it.
Review worker count, logging, and TLS handling before production release.
anti_patterns: Running reload mode in production environments.
Copying public bind examples without considering network exposure.
Treating example certificate paths as a complete TLS security plan.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: It covers Uvicorn usage for ASGI frameworks such as FastAPI, Starlette, Django Channels, and custom ASGI applications.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples only. The user or assistant must decide whether to run any command.

faq.question: Why do examples use all-interface binding?
faq.answer: All-interface binding is common for containers and routed production services. Local development should usually use a local-only bind.

faq.question: Can this skill configure HTTPS?
faq.answer: It explains Uvicorn SSL options and certificate file paths, but it does not create or manage certificates.

faq.question: Can reload and workers be used together?
faq.answer: No. Reload is for development file watching, while workers are for multi-process serving.

faq.question: Does this replace production hardening guidance?
faq.answer: No. It gives Uvicorn-focused server guidance and should be paired with application, platform, and network security review.
